content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Construct an N×NN \times NN×N grid (i.e. NNN rows and NNN columns) filled with N2N^2N2 integers. Let Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j​ represent the element in iii-th row and jjj-th column.

Define Vi,jV_{i, j}Vi,j​ be the set of all elements Ax,yA_{x, y}Ax,y​ such that **exactly one** of x=ix = ix=i and y=jy = jy=j holds. In other words, Vi,jV_{i, j}Vi,j​ is the set of all elements in the same row or column, excluding (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) itself.

The grid must satisfy the following conditions:


0≤Ai,j≤N20 \le A_{i, j} \le N^20≤Ai,j​≤N2
Ai,j=MEX(Vi,j)†A_{i, j} = MEX(V_{i, j}) ^{\dagger}Ai,j​=MEX(Vi,j​)†
It can be proven that a valid grid always exists. Find any valid grid.

†^{\dagger}† MEX(S)MEX(S)MEX(S) is defined as the smallest non-negative integer not present in SSS. The mex of an empty set is defined to be 000.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines each with NNN integers. The jjj-th integer on the iii-th line should be Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j​. You are allowed to print any valid grid.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : V1,1V_{1, 1}V1,1​ is empty, and hence MEX(V1,1)MEX(V_{1, 1})MEX(V1,1​) will always be 000. Thus, we must have A1,1=0A_{1, 1} = 0A1,1​=0.
Test Case 2 : We can verify that the given grid follows all conditions. For example, V1,1={0,0}V_{1, 1} = \{0, 0\}V1,1​={0,0} which has a MEXMEXMEX of 1=A1,11 = A_{1, 1}1=A1,1​.
